head.name: Maria Christina Endarwati
head.bio: Maria Christina Endarwati is one of the professional planners in Indonesia, starting her career in the world of urban and regional planning since 1998 to form a sharp analysis in the field of urban and regional planning. Studying at the National Institute of Technology Malang under the direction of experienced lecturers in the world of planners made her a reliable planner, so in 2000 she decided to take a master’s degree to deepen her knowledge of regional and urban planning at RMIT Australia. After returning to study from Australia, he immediately devoted himself to his beloved campus, Institut Teknologi Nasional Malang. Over time with the achievements made Maria Christina Endarwati was appointed as a Civil Servant by the Batu City government to become the personal secretary of the mayor of Batu. Feeling that it was enough to devote herself to Batu City in the following year decided to resign and refocus on the world of spatial planning in Indonesia and became a permanent lecturer at the National Institute of Technology Malang. Over time with a myriad of experiences as a planner practitioner lecturer in Indonesia led him to become one of the assessors for the Indonesian Planner Expert Association. In addition, he is a member of the Indonesian Planner School Association and is often a speaker at events organized by GFRSD (Global Forum For Sustainability Rural Development). Returning to the world of education, she continued her doctoral program at the faculty of environmental science at Brawaijaya University with a focus on the world of hatred and received her doctorate in 2022 cum laude. And most recently Maria Christina joined the National Capital City development project.

members.name: SARY Tatpaporn
members.bio: President/CEO

members.name: Dr. Gülsün Kurubacak Çakir
members.bio: Dr. Gulsun Kurubacak Cakir received her first undergraduate degree (B.A.) from Ankara University, College of Educational Sciences, Department of Educational Administration and Planning in 1985; she completed her master’s degree (M.A.) in 1987 from Anadolu University Social Sciences Institute Educational Technologies Department and earned her doctorate (Ed.D.) in 2000 from Cincinnati University Teachers College, Department of Curriculum and Instruction. Dr. Kurubacak Cakir was a postdoctoral researcher in Distance Education at the University of New Mexico between 2001 and 2002.Dr. Kurubacak Cakir received her B.S. degree from the Computer Engineering Department of the International Turkish-Kazakhstan University, Faculty of Information Technologies and Engineering in 2013. Dr. Kurubacak Cakir is an Istanbul University College of Open Education Justice Associate Degree Program student.   Dr. Kurubacak Cakir has thirty-five-year experience in open and distance learning systems. She is one of the distance education faculty members of Anadolu University College of Open Education, Department of Distance Education.             Dr. Kurubacak Cakir is also an adjunct professor both at the Faculty of Engineering at Jamia Hamdard University, New Delhi, India, and at the B.A. in Electrical Engineering Education Program, Department of Electrical Engineering, Faculty of Engineering, Universitas Negeri Malang, Malang, Indonesia. Dr. Kurubacak Cakir is the R&D and International Relations Group Coordinator and also she is the Quality Group Coordinator between January 2020-March 2020 at the Anadolu University Open Education System. Dr. Kurubacak Cakir is the founder and chief editor of ETF (Educational Technology Foresight – http://journalpps.um.ac.id/index.php/css/index), AUAd (Journal of Open Education Research and Practice – http://auad.anadolu.edu.tr) since 2015; she is also the assistant editor of TOJDE (Turkish Journal of Online Distance Education – http://tojde.anadolu.edu.tr). Dr. Kurubacak Cakir is a founding Open Education Programs Accreditation and Distance Education Association (AUDAK) member. She was also the chairwoman of AUDAK between 2017-2021Dr. Kurubacak Cakir is also the founder of the @zamninotesineyolculuk (@journeybeyondtime), @uzaktanegitimkocu (@distanceeducarioncoach), @kelebekketkisikocu (@butterflyeffect), and @nitel_okuryazar (@qualitattive_literacy) movements, which broadcasting live programs on the Instagram addresses.   Dr. Gulsun Kurubacak Cakir has received numerous awards for her research interest, including, in 2021, the Gold Ambassador Award and the Club for Leaders, a European Project. She has several publications and projects in open and distance learning. Her research interests include quantitative, qualitative, and mixed research; the design and implementation of an open and learning environment with a transhumanist culture; project-based learning and evaluation; and universal design principles and new technologies (BlockchainTechnologies, Learning Technologies, Foresight, Vision Design, Mobile Technologies, Virtual Reality, Augmented Reality, Mixed Reality, Immersive Learning, Metaverse, Transhumanism).

members.bio: I am CHAN Vicheth, born on 1 January 1969 in Battambang, with present home address at House # 249, Group. 11, Sophy II, Rotanak, Battambang municipality, Battambang Province. For the past thirty-one (31) years, I have worked for ten (11) Non-Governmental Organizations from Thailand to Cambodia and I have earned various work experiences in project and program management in the fields of health, education, disability, child protection, mine action, rural development, climate change and adaptation and environmental protection and biodiversity conservation. I am married. My educational level is MBA and attended various short training courses abroad and in Cambodia mainly on project and program management. For sustainable living, my personal and professional life goal is to promote prosperity, harmony and interdependence between human and nature. I like playing Chapey (khmer Veena) in my free time.

members.name: Puttaroo Amiirah Bibi Rushaa
members.bio: Amiirah is a dedicated environmentalist and academic with a diverse background in sustainability and climate justice. Holding a position as a lecturer and program coordinator at a university, she leverages her extensive education and experience to drive impactful initiatives. Amiirah has represented Mauritius and civil society organizations at numerous international climate events, including those organized by the African Union. She is also the Vice President of the African Network for Policy Research and Advocacy for Sustainability (ANPRAS), spearheading efforts for Earth Hour, Earth Day Network, and Let’s Do It Mauritius. A multilingual advocate fluent in seven languages, Amiirah’s commitment to environmental causes is further demonstrated through her participation in the Nairobi Summer School on Climate Justice, where she has emerged as a prominent voice for climate action and sustainability in the SADC region.

Actuellement je travaille, je travaille avec plusieurs organisations de la Société civile.

members.name: SOSSOU Damien Valère Megan
members.bio: Megan Valère SOSSOU is a dedicated climate leader and agro-climatology expert with a decade of experience in environmental advocacy and sustainable community project management. He holds an advanced degree in Climate, Water, and Environmental Sciences from the University of Abomey-Calavi and an advanced degree in Environmental Analysis and Geospatial Studies from the University of Parakou. His professional journey includes roles such as National Coordinator of the Network of Youth Organizations for Environment and Climate of Benin and Executive Director of the NGO Save Our Planet. Megan has a strong background in leadership, communication, and data analysis, making him a passionate and committed advocate for environmental protection and sustainable development.

members.bio: Kemoneilwe Nchakata is a seasoned professional with a solid educational background and extensive experience in both tourism and human resources. Holding a Bachelor of Business Honours degree in Tourism Management and a Diploma in Human Resources Management and Tourism Management, Kemoneilwe has honed a diverse skill set that bridges these fields. With six years of experience managing accommodation facilities, Kemoneilwe has demonstrated exceptional leadership and operational expertise, ensuring high standards of service and guest satisfaction. Additionally, with three years of experience as an HR professional, Kemoneilwe has effectively managed recruitment, training, and employee relations, contributing to the development of a productive and motivated workforce. This unique blend of experience and education positions Kemoneilwe as a versatile and capable professional, adept at navigating the complexities of both the tourism and human resources sectors.

members.name: Alie kamara
members.bio: I’m Alie Kamara a passionate youth and human rights advocate.  Born in PortLoko District in the Northeast region of Sierra Leone in a smaller community that’s exposed to several forms of child exploitation and abuse and also illiterate youth.  In my early youthful days I left to further my studies at the capital where I sat to the WASSCE exams but couldn’t proceed to the university due to my financial background.  Currently back in my community I’ve be an active human rights advocate and feminist and I’ve worked with different organizations where I served in different capacities. Youth Alliance for Better Future Sierra Leone; i served as country director, Mano River Union Farmers Organization, Regional Coordinator and I’m also  work as a community paralegal to help promote access to justice for community people   In 2021 I did two short term courses with YALI Network on *Community Journalism: Basic Principles for Effective Storytelling* and *Advancing the Role of Women in Politics, Peace and Security, and Economic Institutions*  I’m currently using my skills and knowledge to impact lives and transform communities for a sustainable and reproductive future for all.

members.name: Eman Sobh
members.bio: Dr. Eman Sobh is an assistant professor of Chest Diseases, Faculty of Medicine for Girls, Al-Azhar University, Egypt & College of Medical Rehabilitation Sciences, Taibah University, Saudi Arabia. She is the Director of Science and Communications, African Science Frontiers Initiatives (ASFI), and co-convener of ASFI’s monthly science seminar series.   Dr. Sobh obtained a Master’s degree and Ph.D./Medical Doctorate from Al-Azhar University, Egypt. She is a certified trainer for scientific writing and publishing and soft skills. She is an enthusiastic and motivated person. has a passion for education and capacity building in science and research. She delivered several training programs in soft skills, scientific writing, and publishing and participated in several conferences and scientific events. She has published about 80 journal papers and participated in three book chapters. Her research interests include respiratory diseases, smoking cessation, health education, and capacity building. One of her interests is engaging the public and patients in science and research and she has participated in several campaigns for raising public awareness and health education.

members.name: Dalitso Mndinda
members.bio: I I have a diverse and extensive background in community development, environmental management, and youth empowerment, primarily working with various governmental departments in Malawi.  My experience includes roles such as District Community Development Officer with the Ministry of Gender, Community Development, and Social Welfare, where I planned and guided community development activities, coordinated social protection programs, and facilitated resource mobilization.   I also served as Acting Environmental District Officer for the Ministry of Environment, Mining, and Natural Resources, focusing on environmental protection, impact assessments, and education campaigns.  Additionally, I acted as District Youth Officer for the Ministry of Youth and Sports, where I organized youth development initiatives and provided guidance on policy implementation.   My educational background includes a Postgraduate Diploma in Rural Development Management from the National Institute of Rural Development Management and Panchayat Raj, a BSc in Development Studies from Pentecostal Life University, a Diploma in Food, Nutrition, and Livelihood Security from Lilongwe University of Agriculture and Natural Resources, and a Certificate in Community Development from Magomero Community Development College.

members.name: Olabanji Oluwafemi Peter
members.bio: Dr. Olabanji Oluwafemi Peter is an outstanding researcher based in Ilorin, Kwara State Nigeria. He had his M.Sc. in Agricultural Extension and Rural Development with distinction and earned a Doctorate degree in Agricultural Extension from the University of Ilorin, Ilorin, Kwara State, Nigeria. As a good team player and result oriented person, Dr Olabanji through his expertise in module and survey instrument development and vast knowledge in general agriculture has been part of a number of developmental research conducted in Nigeria. He was in the research team that implemented innovative research on harnessing the Potentials of Agricultural Science Teachers in Agricultural Advisory Service Delivery in Neglected Areas of Kwara State under Prime Agreement with African Forum for Agricultural Advisory Service (AFAAS) funded by European Union. Dr Olabanji is currently implementing Activity 3 of a USDA funded five-year West Africa Cashew Project in Nigeria as team lead. Dr Olabanji has his core research interest in community development and agricultural innovation studies. He has publications in over nineteen reputable journals both national and international. Dr Olabanji loves researching, technical presentations, and Table tennis.

members.name: Jean Marie Habarugira
members.bio: Jean Marie is an experienced business professional currently working for Bboxx, a data-driven super platform that deploys innovative products across Africa, connecting consumers to clean, reliable, and affordable energy. Bboxx is positively impacting the lives of more than 3.7 million people across 11 markets. Bboxx and its group of companies form one of the largest networks in Africa, with over 4,000 staff across Africa and offices in the UK and Asia. The company aims to reach 36 million people by 2028. Jean holds a bachelor’s degree in Healthcare Management with a concentration in Global Perspectives from Southern New Hampshire University (USA). He also co-founded Act for a Better Future, a not-for-profit organization committed to education, equality, and inclusion, which can significantly advance the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) by ensuring access to quality education (SDG 4), promoting gender equality (SDG 5), and reducing inequalities (SDG 10). This involves providing scholarships, developing training programs, supporting diverse hiring practices, and fostering a safe, inclusive work environment. By focusing on these areas, the organization contributes to creating a more equitable, inclusive, and educated society.

members.name: Dr. Abduselam Abdulahi Mohamed
members.bio: Abduselam Abdulahi Mohamed is one of the key figures/founding members at Kebri Dehar University; he is serving as vice president for academic and research affairs and as an assistant professor at Kebri Dehar University. He obtained both his bachelor’s and master’s degrees in Agricultural Economics from Jimma University, and Ambo University (Ethiopia), respectively. He did his PhD in Managerial Economics from Indian Management School and Research Institute, and PhD in Development Studies from University of South Africa (UNISA). Abduselam A. Mohamed received Mini-MBA in Leadership from European Institute of Leadership & Management, Diploma in Managerial Economics from Alison-Group London, Higher Diploma Program Certificate from Haramaya University (Ethiopia). He is a certified master’s Class in teaching online from Arizona State University-ASU (United States), and Specialized Management Consulting  graduate from Emory University (United States, Atlanta). Abduselam’s rich academic journey is complemented by roles as a graduate assistant lecturer at Raya University and an expert in the Revenue Bureau, Somali Regional State, member of the boarding directors for Kebridahar Teacher Taning College under the regional educational bureau. Prior to his current position, he served in different roles at Kebri Dehar University, including acting vice president for academic and research affairs, director for the student services directorate, director for the research and development directorate, member of the university academic council, chairman of the research and publication ethics committee, and member of the university management council. He is a founder and CEO of Sahan Development Consulting Firm (SAHANDEVCON), a multidisciplinary consulting firm based in Somali regional state, Ethiopia. Abduselam Abdulahi is actively participating in different national and international professional associations and activities serving as Global Forum for Sustainable Rural Development (Country Director-Ethiopia), Full member in Ethiopian Economic Association, International Society for Development and Sustainability (Japan), African Association of Agricultural Economists, Ethiopian Biodiversity Conservation Society, International Association of Agricultural Economists, KDU, representing members of the Association of African Universities, Reviewer of the International Journal of Agricultural Economics, Reviewer of Hindawi Journals, and Reviewer of Greener Scholarly Publisher. Also, Abduselam Abdulahi is active in research and interested in development issues with institutional, regional, and national significance. His research interests include food security analysis, development program impact evaluation, pastoralism and livelihood analysis, development studies, labor economics, managerial economics, the economics of food security, drought and climate change, agricultural and rural development issues, agricultural policy, agribusiness and marketing, peace and security, resource-based conflict and associated factors, youth unemployment, rural-urban migration, public policy, and public affairs, among others. Abduselam has published several research articles with high impact in international, reputable scientific journals.
